title: Garlicky Almond Brazilian Stir-fry
description: A tender and energizing garlicky dish with almond essence, made pressure-cook style for a quick meal that's also jain-friendly.

ingredients.name: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: adjust based on preference for spiciness

instructions: Preheat the pressure cooker according to manufacturer’s instructions.
Crush and finely chop the garlic cloves. Crush almonds lightly with a rolling pin or in a food processor until coarsely chopped.
In a separate pan, sauté crushed garlic over medium heat for about 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add broccoli florets and bell pepper to the pan with garlic. Sauté for another minute until slightly softened.
Push all ingredients towards one side of the pan and pour in almond pieces into the other half. Let them toast lightly for a couple seconds.
Pour in about 2 tablespoons of water, seal the pressure cooker, and cook on high pressure for approximately 15 minutes or as per cooker instructions.
Quick release the steam (this could be done by opening the valve slightly first then closing to let some steam escape before fully opening).
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Remove from heat and serve hot with your favorite side dish.
